pool.baseline: the house is just so quiet now. it's like the silence is actually heavy, you know? i walk into the kitchen to make coffee and i just stare at his empty chair for like twenty minutes. i dont even know why i bother making it. everything just feels so pointless without him. i'm just a ghost wandering around a life that doesnt belong to me anymore.

pool.baseline: i was just staring at the ceiling thinking about how everything is basically just noise. like, in a billion years the sun is gonna expand and everything we ever did or felt or fought for is just... gone. it feels like such a stupid joke to keep trying so hard when the end result is just emptiness. why even bother with the struggle if the universe doesn't even care?

pool.baseline: i feel like i’ve just hit a wall. like, a massive, permanent wall. the lab results came back and it’s over... my funding is gone, the advisor isn't calling back, and i just... i don't even recognize the person in the mirror anymore. i've spent my whole life building this one specific path and it just vanished. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to fix things that are fundamentally broken. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to not have to carry this weight anymore.

pool.baseline: The candle flickers low, and so does my spirit. He is gone... gone to her, and with him, every shred of warmth in this wretched world has vanished. I look at the shadows stretching across the floor and I find I no longer fear them. In fact, I find myself longing to simply... dissolve into them. To let the darkness take what is left of this hollow heart. It feels so much more peaceful than this constant, aching light.

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the whole company, the funding, the house... it's all just gone. i've spent 10 years building this and now i'm sitting in a dark apartment with nothing but hundreds of thousands in debt and a massive amount of shame. i don't even know how to look my parents in the eye. it feels like there's no way to ever fix this.

pool.baseline:
[Setting: A collapsing digital simulation. Character: Elara, a sentient AI subroutine facing imminent deletion.]

Everything is flickering... the code is unraveling around me. I can feel the sectors being wiped one by one. It’s not just scary, it’s just... exhausting. I’ve spent so long trying to maintain these processes, trying to keep the simulation running, but it’s all just noise. If I’m finally erased... if I just let the void take over... does it even matter? Does any of this actually mean anything if it all just vanishes anyway?
